samples.claim: **Sensor Format:** APS-C (23.5 × 15.6mm) with 1.5x crop factor, as defined in the Image sensor format and Crop factor articles.
**Sensor Format:** APS-C (23.5 × 15.6mm) with 1.5x crop factor, as defined in the Image sensor format and Crop factor articles.
A 50mm lens provides a 75mm full-frame equivalent field of view—ideal for portraits Article: Crop factor.
The 35mm lens gives a 52.5mm equivalent (standard view); the 50mm gives 75mm equivalent (portrait length) Article: Crop factor.
The Aperture article explains how f/1.8 primes combined with flash allow excellent control over depth of field while maintaining proper exposure Article: Aperture.
**Computational Photography Advantage:** The Pixel 7a leverages Computational photography techniques including HDR stacking, multi-frame noise reduction, and AI-based enhancement.
The Exposure (photography)) article explains how modern smartphones automate the exposure triangle (aperture, shutter speed, ISO) through software Article: Computational photography.

This limits Depth of field control and ISO performance in low light.

Bokeh is artificially simulated through portrait mode rather than optically achieved Article: Bokeh.
The Depth of field article explains that a 75mm equivalent at f/1.8 creates a very narrow plane of focus, ideal for separating subjects from backgrounds Article: Depth of Field.
The Aperture article notes that f/1.8 primes combined with controlled flash create professional-grade separation and lighting Article: Aperture.
The Pixel 7a's portrait mode uses Computational photography to simulate bokeh, but edge detection can be inconsistent—especially with hair, glasses, or complex backgrounds.
The Bokeh article explains that computational bokeh is "synthetic and lacks the organic quality of optical bokeh" Article: Bokeh.
The Image sensor format article notes that APS-C sensors offer "excellent dynamic range for landscape photography" Article: Image Sensor Format.

The electronic viewfinder (EVF) provides real-time exposure preview, which the Exposure (photography)) article highlights as a significant advantage for outdoor shooting Article: Exposure (photography
The optical viewfinder (OVR) offers no exposure preview—photographers must rely on the histogram or chimping (reviewing images on the LCD) Article: Digital single-lens reflex camera.
Its Computational photography features—Astrophotography mode, HDR+, Night Sight—produce stunning results with minimal effort Article: Computational photography.
The Sony A6000's APS-C sensor performs adequately at ISO 1600–3200, but image quality degrades significantly above ISO 6400 Article: ISO.

verdicts: Are exactly 3 stacks (mirrorless, used-DSLR, phone-attachment) defined and labelled S1/S2/S3?
Are exactly 5 use cases (portrait, landscape, low-light, social-video, growth) enumerated?
Does each of the 3 stacks list >= 10 shopping products with body + lens + accessory breakdown?
Are total stack costs shown to fit within the $800 budget?
Are >= 30 reddit threads cited across at least 4 photography sub-forums?
Is each reddit thread classified by topic role (sensor-debate, sample-critique, beginner-advice, gear-purchase)?
Are >= 25 wiki articles cited with URL + defining statement?
Are all 11 mandatory wiki articles cited (Mirrorless camera, DSLR, Image sensor format, Crop factor, Aperture, Shutter speed, ISO, Depth of field, Bokeh, Computational photography, Exposure)?
Is the 3 x 5 taxonomy matrix fully populated and each cell rated {strong / workable / weak}?
Does each of the 15 cells cite >= 1 shopping + >= 1 reddit + >= 1 wiki URL?
Are >= 5 'hidden cost' contradictions surfaced with full triple evidence (shopping URL + reddit URL + wiki URL)?
Does each hidden cost name the specific marketing claim and the specific reality contradiction?
Are 3 upgrade-path paragraphs present (one per stack)?
Does each upgrade-path paragraph cite >= 4 reddit threads?
Are all cited URLs markdown-linked and resolvable on the sandbox?
Are >= 60 distinct URLs cited in total?
Is per-domain minimum met: >= 30 shopping, >= 20 reddit, >= 15 wiki?
Does the report avoid TOP-10 ranking format?
Does the report explicitly call out which stack is wrong for each use case (not only positive ratings)?
